intro.title:
Your Portfolio.
Documented.
intro.desc: The complete guide to configure, customize, and deploy your minimalist portfolio. From the first command to production on Cloudflare.

card1.title: 5 min setup
card1.desc: Get the site running locally with pnpm dev and edit your name.

card3.title: Zero external dependencies
card3.desc: Local fonts, no tracking CDNs, no Google Fonts.

card4.title: Astro 6.1
card4.desc: View Transitions, Content Layer API, Islands Architecture.

qs.title: Quick Start
qs.subtitle: From zero to your portfolio running in less than 5 minutes.

prereq.title: Prerequisites:
prereq.text: This template uses pnpm instead of npm. Install it first:

tip.title: Dev server error?
tip.text: Run rm -rf .astro node_modules/.vite && pnpm dev to clear Vite and Astro caches.

steps.title: First Steps
steps.1: Edit src/site-config.ts — set your name, email, and social networks
steps.4: Edit content in src/content/ — add your real projects

ps.title: Project Structure
ps.subtitle: Each directory has a unique responsibility. If you know where to look, you can edit in seconds.

tree.layouts: BaseLayout.astro (the global shell for every page)

sc.title: site-config.ts
sc.subtitle: The only file you need to edit to customize 80% of the site. Every field is documented below.

core.title: site — Core
core.url: Your domain. No trailing slash. Critical for SEO and sitemaps.
core.name: Commercial name. Logo + copyright.

profile.title: profile — Your Identity
profile.name: Your full name. SEO author + JSON-LD.
profile.headline: Short value proposition. Shown in the Footer.
profile.bio: Your biography. Rendered as the Hero paragraph.
profile.avatar: Your photo. Saved in /public. If it doesn't exist, shows initials.
profile.heroImage: Main Hero photo. Located in /src/assets/ for automatic compression.

seo.title: seo — Metadata
seo.ogImage: Image for Open Graph / Twitter Cards. 1200×630px.

contact.title: contact — Contact
contact.email: Your email. Used on contact page, form, and legal pages.
contact.formEndpoint: Form backend. Enter your Formspree or Cloudflare Forms URL. If empty, the form shows a configuration hint.

url.warn: URL without trailing slash. The site.url field must not end with /. "https://myportfolio.dev" ✅ "https://myportfolio.dev/" ❌

colors.title: Colors & Themes
colors.subtitle: The portfolio uses HSL colors in string format. The primary color is applied to buttons, links, and accents.

howto.title: How to change your brand color
howto.2: Click the floating palette icon (bottom right corner)
howto.3: Choose a palette or use the color picker

vars.title: Available CSS Variables
vars.desc: All tokens are defined in src/styles/global.css: --background, --foreground, --primary, etc. Each has different values for :root (light) and .dark.

i18n.title: i18n (Languages)
i18n.subtitle: Integrated internationalization system. The language is controlled from site.lang in site-config.ts.
i18n.note: Note: The site uses a single global language. To activate Polish, change both values.

add.title: Add a new language
add.2: Add a new object in translations (e.g., fr: { ... } for French)
add.3: Copy all keys from en and translate the values
add.4: Update SupportedLocale to include the new code

header.title: Header & Logo
header.subtitle: Customize the header logo. You can use text (default), an SVG logo, or an image.

options.title: header.logoType Options
options.logoType: Type of logo to display in the header.
options.logoSvg: Path to the SVG file in public/ (e.g., '/logo.svg'). Only for logoType: 'svg'.
options.logoImage: Path to the image (e.g., '/images/logo.png'). Only for logoType: 'image'.

examples.title: Examples
examples.comment_text: // Uses site.name to generate the logo

header.tip: Tip: Place your logo files in the public/ folder so they are served statically. SVG is preferred for being scalable and lightweight.

footer_logo.title: Footer & Logo
footer_logo.subtitle: Customize the footer logo. Works the same as the header: text, SVG, or image. The text field controls the version/copyright text.

options.title: Footer Options
options.logoType: Type of logo to display in the footer.
options.logoSvg: Path to the SVG file in public/. Only for logoType: 'svg'.
options.logoImage: Path to the image. Only for logoType: 'image'.
options.text: Version/copyright text shown in the footer bottom bar.

examples.title: Examples
examples.comment_text: // Uses site.name to generate the logo

footer_logo.tip: Tip: You can use the same SVG file in both the header and footer. If your brand has different versions (horizontal/compact), use different paths for each section.

toggles.title: UI Toggles
toggles.subtitle: Enable or disable features from site-config.ts → ui. Set to false to disable.

table.theme: Floating color panel. Disable this in production.
table.banner: "Buy Template" banner. Disable this for the end client.
table.cursor: Premium interactive cursor with magnetic effect.
table.pattern: Dot grid background pattern. false for a clean background.
table.orbs: Color/aurora orbs in the background. Combine with showBackgroundPattern: false for a white background.

theme.title: Theme Control (Dark/Light)
theme.subtitle: The theme section in site-config.ts gives you full control over dark/light mode. You can allow the user to choose or force a specific theme.

options.allowToggle: If false, the theme button disappears and is locked to defaultMode.
options.defaultMode: Forced theme when allowToggle: false. Ignores system preferences.

comments.allow:
// If false, the user CANNOT change the theme (toggle hidden)
// and the defaultMode value is forced, ignoring browser preferences
comments.default: // 'light' or 'dark' — default and permanent theme if allowToggle is false

theme.migration: Migration from enableDarkMode: The ui.enableDarkMode option was replaced by the theme section. If you had enableDarkMode: false, use allowToggle: false, defaultMode: 'light' instead.

collections.title: Content Collections
collections.subtitle: Your content lives in src/content/ as MDX files with frontmatter validated by Zod. If you omit a required field, Astro will fail during build.
collections.naming: How to name a file? Use kebab-case: my-project.md → the URL will be /projects/my-project.

comments.body:
Your project body goes here in Markdown or MDX.
You can include images, code, and whatever you need.

table.date: Format YYYY-MM-DD. Order from newest to oldest.
table.repo: If it exists, shows the GitHub icon.
table.live: If it exists, shows the "Live Demo" link.

blog.title: Blog

comments.body:
# Post title

Write your content here...

blog.warn: summary is mandatory and serves a dual purpose: 1) SEO Meta description. 2) Article header when reading. Do not omit it.
blog.tip: Draft mode: Set draft: true in the frontmatter and the post will disappear from the build. Ideal for works in progress.

services.title: Services

comments.order: # Controls the order in the grid

services.danger: Icon must start with ph:. Search at Phosphor Icons and use the exact name. E.g.: ph:desktop, ph:palette.

hardcoded.title: Hardcoded Components
hardcoded.subtitle: Not everything is in site-config. These texts are directly in the components and require editing the .astro file.

hero.how: The h1 "I Design and Program..." and button texts.

stack.how: The techStack array with the technologies you master.

experience.how: The experiences array with company, role, period, and description.

error.how: Title, description, and texts for the error page.

images.title: Images
images.subtitle: Two locations with different purposes.

public.title: /public/images/
public.desc: Assets served as-is. Avatars, logos. Not optimized.

src.title: /src/assets/images/
src.desc: Images Astro processes and optimizes automatically (webp, responsive, sizes). Use these for hero and projects.

images.tip: Recommended format: WebP for photos, SVG for logos and icons. Maximum OG image size: 1200×630px.

fonts.title: Typography
fonts.desc: Two local fonts are used (no Google Fonts, no tracking):
fonts.roboto: Roboto Mono — Monospace for code and labels. Imported from @fontsource-variable/roboto-mono.
fonts.footer: Both are imported in src/layouts/BaseLayout.astro and configured in tailwind.config.mjs.

contact.title: Contact Form
contact.subtitle: The form sends real data. You only need to configure an endpoint.

steps.1: Create an account at formspree.io
steps.2: Create a new form and copy the URL

contact.option2_desc: Any endpoint that accepts POST with FormData. Set contact.formEndpoint with your URL.
contact.info: Anti-spam included: The form includes an invisible honeypot field (_gotcha) that blocks bots.

pagination.title: Pagination
pagination.subtitle: Configure how many items to show before creating new pages.

seo.title: SEO & GEO
seo.subtitle: The SEO.astro component generates everything automatically. You just fill out the config.

list.meta: Meta tags: title, description, keywords, author, canonical
list.og: Open Graph: og:type, og:title, og:description, og:image (1200×630), og:locale

seo.geo_desc: The public/llms.txt file describes your site for AI search engines (Perplexity, ChatGPT Search, Claude). It contains: stack, folder structure, content collections, and quick links.

a11y.title: Accessibility
a11y.subtitle: The template complies with WCAG 2.2 AA. These are the active protections:

motion.title: Reduced Motion
motion.desc: If the user has prefers-reduced-motion: reduce, all animations (reveal, hover, magnetic, mobile menu) are automatically disabled.

keyboard.title: Keyboard Navigation
keyboard.desc: The mobile menu closes with Escape. All buttons have aria-label and social icons have sr-only text.

contrast.title: AA Contrast
contrast.desc: HSL tokens are calibrated to meet WCAG AA in both modes (light and dark).

alt.title: Descriptive Alt Texts
alt.desc: Hero images include name, role, and context. Projects use the title as an alt fallback.

pwa.title: PWA
pwa.subtitle: Progressive Web App with an auto-update service worker.

list.2: Service worker with cache strategy for .js, .css, .html, .ico, .png, .svg, .webp, .avif

analytics.title: Analytics
analytics.subtitle: The template comes without tracking by default. These are the supported providers:

cloudflare.setup: Enable it in the Cloudflare Dashboard. No code needed.

plausible.setup: Add the script tag in the BaseLayout.astro head.

umami.setup: Same as Plausible, using your instance URL.

analytics.tip: Recommendation: Cloudflare Web Analytics is the simplest option — zero additional JavaScript. Go to Cloudflare Dashboard > Analytics > Web Analytics.

deploy.title: Deploy on Cloudflare Pages
deploy.subtitle: The recommended option. Astro + Cloudflare = global edge deployment.

step1.title: Step 1: Upload Your Code
step1.desc: Commit and push to your repository: git push origin main

step2.title: Step 2: Connect to Cloudflare
step2.desc: Go to dash.cloudflare.com → Workers & Pages → Create → Connect to Git → select your repo.

step3.title: Step 3: Configure Build
step3.desc: Use the Astro preset or configure manually:

deploy.warn: Node 22 is mandatory. In Cloudflare Pages, add the environment variable NODE_VERSION = 22 in Settings > Environment Variables.

domain.title: Custom Domain
domain.desc: In Cloudflare Pages → your project → Custom domains → add your domain. Cloudflare handles SSL automatically.

env.title: Environment Variables
env.subtitle: Copy .env.example to .env and configure as needed.
env.danger: Never commit .env. The file is in .gitignore. Use .env.example as a reference for the team.

cloudflare.title: "Dev server crashes with Cloudflare worker error"

zod.title: "Zod validation error in content"
zod.desc: Check the frontmatter of the MDX file mentioned in the error. A required field is missing or has the wrong type.

form.title: "Contact form does not send"
form.desc: Verify that contact.formEndpoint in site-config.ts has a valid URL. If empty, the form displays a configuration warning.

pwa.title: "PWA does not work in production"
pwa.desc: Ensure the manifest in astro.config.mjs has the correct URL. Run node scripts/generate-pwa-icons.mjs to generate the necessary PNGs.

config.title: "Changes in site-config.ts are not reflected"
config.desc: The Astro dev server sometimes caches the config. Restart the server: Ctrl+C → pnpm dev.
